nodes.x: O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.
O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!
Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: let thy glory be above all the earth.
Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: and thy glory above all the earth;
Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens; let thy glory be above all the earth.
Let them praise the name of the Lord: for his name alone is excellent; his glory is above the earth and heaven.
Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings hast thou ordained strength because of thine enemies, that thou mightest still the enemy and the avenger.

And number thee an army, like the army that thou hast lost, horse for horse, and chariot for chariot: and we will fight against them in the plain, and surely we shall be stronger than they. And he hearkened unto their voice, and did so.

Nevertheless, thine enemy is in thine hands; and if thou rewardest him according to his works thou art justified; if he has sought thy life, and thy life is endangered by him, thine enemy is in thine hands and thou art justified.
For thou art the God of my strength: why dost thou cast me off? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
For they speak against thee wickedly, and thine enemies take thy name in vain.
Let thine anger be kindled against our enemies; and, in the fury of thine heart, with thy sword avenge us of our wrongs.
When I consider thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained;
Of old hast thou laid the foundation of the earth: and the heavens are the work of thy hands.
And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth; and the heavens are the works of thine hands:

Gratitude By Elder Lloyd P. George Of the Seventy The Psalmist asked the question: “When I consider thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained; “What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him? “For thou hast made him a little lower than the angels, and hast crowned him with glory and honour. “Thou madest him to…

And lest thou lift up thine eyes unto heaven, and when thou seest the sun, and the moon, and the stars, even all the host of heaven, shouldest be driven to worship them, and serve them, which the Lord thy God hath divided unto all nations under the whole heaven.
And let the beauty of the Lord our God be upon us: and establish thou the work of our hands upon us; yea, the work of our hands establish thou it.
What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?
But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is man, that thou art mindful of him? or the son of man, that thou visitest him?
Lord, what is man, that thou takest knowledge of him! or the son of man, that thou makest account of him!

Also, thou son of man, shall it not be in the day when I take from them their strength, the joy of their glory, the desire of their eyes, and that whereupon they set their minds, their sons and their daughters,
¶ And he came unto his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here am I; who art thou, my son?
thee from iniquity, and that thou shouldst become to Him a son, and a servant, and a minister of His presence.
For thou hast made him a little lower than the angels, and hast crowned him with glory and honour.
Thou madest him a little lower than the angels; thou crownedst him with glory and honour, and didst set him over the works of thy hands:
Thou hast made his glory to cease, and cast his throne down to the ground.
His glory is great in thy salvation: honour and majesty hast thou laid upon him.
and stand upon the earth with power and tions of the world; tho angels shall uphold great glory. him; and that ho shall go forth according (
Thou madest him to have dominion over the works of thy hands; thou hast put all things under his feet:
Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet. For in that he put all in subjection under him, he left nothing that is not put under him. But now we see not yet all things put under him.
Hast not thou made an hedge about him, and about his house, and about all that he hath on every side? thou hast blessed the work of his hands, and his substance is increased in the land.
Thou hast set up the right hand of his adversaries; thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice.
For he hath put all things under his feet. But when he saith all things are put under him, it is manifest that he is excepted, which did put all things under him.
Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands.
All sheep and oxen, yea, and the beasts of the field;
These are the beasts which ye shall eat: the ox, the sheep, and the goat,
They took their sheep, and their oxen, and their asses, and that which was in the city, and that which was in the field,
And the consecrated things were six hundred oxen and three thousand sheep.

I stood in the midst of a vast field, sun-rounded by an immense wilderness interwoven with lakes, rivers, and streams. The field and wilderness were fulled with lions, tigers, bears, wolves, and all manner of wild beasts; also, horned cattle, horses, camels, dromedaries, mules, asses, goats, and all species of animals. I looked and beheld some sheep scattered abroad through all the field and…
The fowl of the air, and the fish of the sea, and whatsoever passeth through the paths of the seas.
And I, God, said: Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl which may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.
And the Gods said: Let us prepare the waters to bring forth abundantly the moving creatures that have life; and the fowl, that they may fly above the earth in the open expanse of heaven.
And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.
And they did also lay snares and catch fowls of the air; and they did also prepare a vessel, in which they did carry with them the fish of the waters.
O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!
O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.
O Lord our God, other lords beside thee have had dominion over us: but by thee only will we make mention of thy name.
Now therefore, our God, we thank thee, and praise thy glorious name.

thy name shall have the honor, the praise and glory, for we ask all in Jesus' name. And unto thee and our blessed Lord and Savior and to the Holy Spirit be all power, might and dominion, worlds without end. Amen.
My mouth shall speak the praise of the Lord: and let all flesh bless his holy name for ever and ever.
